message: I'll be honest — when I heard the winter storm warning last year, my first instinct was to panic-scroll through supplies I didn't have. Then my partner said, 'Why don't we just... make it a thing?' So we did. We pulled out board games, made a cozy corner with blankets, charged everything that needed charging (phones, laptop, that weird battery pack we never use), filled some water jugs, and basically turned a potentially stressful day into what felt like a snow day from childhood. Sounds small, but it totally changed how I approached storm prep. Now I actually *want* to get ready instead of dreading it. It's less "bracing for impact" and more "let's make sure we're comfortable together." And honestly? That mindset shift made the actual prep stick — we do it before every season now. Have you found a way to make your storm prep feel less like a chore and more like... well, something that doesn't feel like a chore?
